response.justification: Szabo brings solid staff-level engineering credentials, strong TypeScript proficiency, and notably, a current role explicitly working with AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic workflows—signaling trajectory into GenAI. However, he significantly underperforms the stated core requirements. The position demands 8+ years building production-grade AI solutions; Szabo has approximately 1 year documented in GenAI/AI-infrastructure (current role only), with remaining years in general software engineering across diverse domains. Python is listed as a required skill and is entirely absent from his resume. While his recent focus on MCP and agentic systems demonstrates domain awareness and his full-stack capability could be valuable, the gap between a generalist staff engineer and a staff AI systems engineer is substantial, though his current trajectory warrants exploration.
strengths: Current role (re:cinq, April 2025–present) directly involves AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic workflows—hands-on experience with required GenAI domain and frameworks
Demonstrated staff-level maturity with proven leadership, mentoring ability, and full-stack engineering capability across multiple domains and technologies
Strong TypeScript/Node.js foundation, AWS infrastructure experience (AWS CDK), and proven ability to architect high-performance systems (Dunnhumby: millions of requests per second)
concerns: Python is explicitly listed as a required skill but is entirely absent from resume; experience is exclusively JavaScript/TypeScript, DLang, C++, Java, PHP, and Swift

Does not meet the explicit '8+ years building and shipping production-grade AI solutions' requirement; only ~1 year documented in GenAI/AI-infrastructure, with remaining 8+ years in general software engineering
No documented experience with vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM performance metrics, or multi-agent frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI); MCP work is recent with limited demonstrated depth

key_factors.factor: 8-year AI solutions experience gap vs. requirement
